meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-impact portfolio that proves your technical authority to investors.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, use AI to rapidly engineer high-complexity features that demonstrate depth. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative debugging, you can transform broken MVP prototypes into polished, production-ready case studies that showcase your technical proficiency and speed to market.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the founder’s greatest adversary. You have the vision, but your GitHub profile is a ghost town, and your lack of a public portfolio is killing your conversion rates with potential investors and early adopters. They don't just want to see a landing page; they want to see the scars of battle—the complex architectural decisions that only a seasoned builder makes.

Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project. We are going to weaponize AI-assisted debugging to turn your current MVP, however chaotic, into a masterclass of engineering. This isn't just about fixing bugs; it's about refactoring your codebase into a narrative that proves you can build, scale, and ship under pressure.

steps.title: Architectural Audit
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, map your technical debt. Use Antigravity to visualize your dependency graph and identify 'hotspots' that signal complex logic, which makes for better portfolio storytelling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and map the dependency graph. Identify the top 3 most complex modules that demonstrate advanced architectural patterns suitable for a senior-level portfolio showcase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ls -R --ignore 'node_modules' && claude ask 'Analyze the architectural complexity of this repository.'

steps.title: Automated Bug Reproduction
steps.description: Create a reproducible test case for your most critical failure. This demonstrates your commitment to stability, a key trait investors look for in technical founders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the identified high-complexity module. Focus on edge-case error handling and state consistency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a reproduction script for the current auth state bug'

steps.title: Intelligent Refactoring
steps.description: Use Claude Code to refactor spaghetti code into clean, modular components. Document the 'before' and 'after' to include in your public case study.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified module to follow Clean Architecture principles. Prioritize dependency injection and interface segregation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Refactor the data fetching layer into a clean repository pattern'

steps.title: Performance Optimization Showcase
steps.description: Optimize a critical path. Showing you can squeeze performance out of a stack proves you understand the underlying mechanics of the tools you use.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the execution trace of the core business logic. Suggest and implement 3 optimizations to reduce latency by at least 20%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'profile the current request lifecycle and suggest performance improvements'

steps.title: Documenting the Narrative
steps.description: Use the AI to generate a 'Technical Deep Dive' blog post based on your commits and refactors. This turns your debugging process into a marketing asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the refactors performed, write a technical post-mortem that highlights the challenges, the architectural decisions made, and the performance impact.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Summarize the technical challenges faced and the solutions implemented for a developer-focused blog post'

pro_tips: Always commit your refactored code with atomic, descriptive messages; it creates a clean audit trail for potential technical due diligence.
Use Antigravity to generate visual flowcharts of your logic; these are perfect for inserting into your 'About' page to demonstrate system design skills.
When debugging with Claude Code, ask it to explain the 'why' behind a fix to ensure you can defend the decision in a live interview or demo.
Focus on fixing bugs in the 'happy path' of your app first; this maximizes the impact of your demo for users.
Turn your debugging sessions into 'Build Logs' on Twitter/X; showing the struggle of building is often more compelling than the finished product.
content.next_steps: Take the refactored modules from this session and deploy them as a micro-service or a standalone open-source utility on GitHub to solidify your public portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to transform your messy MVP into a high-authority technical showcase. Stop hiding your code and start leading the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
